title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
summary: Google Kubernetes Engine has added innovations for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. The platform now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads with improved reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness.
writeup: This Google Cloud Tech video surveys recent additions to Google Kubernetes Engine for AI training, serving, and agentic workflows. GKE now supports reinforcement learning and complex AI workloads, with stated improvements in reliability, scalability, and cost-effectiveness. The discussion also touches model compression alongside the core Kubernetes foundation. The takeaway is that GKE is positioning itself to run the full range of AI work, from training through serving to agents, on one platform.
